differential_diagnoses: Dabrafenib and trametinib-induced systemic hypersensitivity (pyrexia syndrome)
Acute interstitial nephritis (secondary to drug reaction or infection)
next_steps: Perform peripheral blood smear to evaluate for morulae and cytopenias
Order laboratory panel for HLH: ferritin, triglycerides, fibrinogen, soluble IL-2 receptor, and LDH
Order tickborne disease PCR panel including Anaplasma phagocytophilum and Borrelia burgdorferi
Order morning cortisol and ACTH levels
Perform renal ultrasound to assess for acute tubular necrosis or interstitial nephritis
Perform imaging with non-contrast MRI or cautious contrast-enhanced CT to evaluate for abdominal pathology
